**Our Commitment to Diversity** 




 SAVR is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome people from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We believe diverse teams build better products and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive.'
 



  





**Ready to Join Us?** 




 We’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out with any questions — and let’s build something meaningful together.

title: Technical Sales Representative– Datacenter Sales, SMB
description:
**Technical Sales Representative – Datacenter Sales, SMB**
 Join us as a Technical Sales Representative on our Data Center Sales team in Sweden to do the best work of your career and make a profound social impact.
   

  

**What you’ll achieve**
 As a Technical Sales Representative (TSR), you will be responsible for supporting datacenter sales opportunities in the SMB segment by providing technical expertise and shaping customer-centric solutions. You will work with account managers, partners, and customers to align Dell’s datacenter portfolio with business needs and drive successful sales outcomes.
   

  

 You will:
   

  

* Support the sales team with technical expertise in customer-facing datacenter opportunities within the SMB segment
* Engage with customers to understand infrastructure, pain points, and future needs, and recommend appropriate solutions
* Deliver technical presentations, demonstrations, and workshops, translating technology into business value
* Collaborate with internal specialists and partners to design competitive solutions and support proposals
* Drive opportunity progression by addressing technical objections and contributing to pipeline growth


  

  

**Take the first step towards your dream career**
 Every Dell Technologies team member brings something unique to the table. Here’s what we are looking for with this role:
   

  

**Essential Requirements**
* Experience in technical sales, presales, or solution advisory roles within IT infrastructure or datacenter technology
* Knowledge of datacenter technologies such as servers, storage, virtualization, backup and recovery, and hyperconverged infrastructure
* Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to technical and non-technical audiences
* Proven ability to understand customer business needs and map them to technical solutions
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience, and fluency in Swedish and English


  

  

**Desirable Requirements**
* Experience working with SMB customers and/or partner-led sales environments
* Familiarity with Dell Technologies datacenter portfolio, hybrid cloud, or infrastructure transformation discussions


  

  

**Who we are**
 We believe that each of us has the power to make an impact. That’s why we put our team members at the center of everything we do. If you’re looking for an opportunity to grow your career with some of the best minds and most advanced tech in the industry, we’re looking for you.
   

  

 Dell Technologies is a unique family of businesses that helps individuals and organizations transform how they work, live and play. Join us to build a future that works for everyone because Progress Takes All of Us here.
   

  

 Dell Technologies is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Read the full Equal Employment Opportunity Policy here.

title: Tech Platform Owner - SAP Operations till Axfood IT, Stockholm
description:
**Samtliga ansökningar behandlas under augusti 2026, i samband med rekryteringsprocessens start.**
 Välkommen till Axfood IT, en av matbranschens största IT-avdelningar med nästan 400 medarbetare. Det är vi som ser till att Axfood, och alla våra kedjor, kan ligga i framkant vad gäller digital utveckling. Vi jobbar med att utveckla och förbättra allt ifrån e-handel till logistiklösningar och AI.
   

  

 Hos Axfood IT blir du del av ett sammanhang som utmanar och levererar lösningar som gör skillnad, både för våra kollegor och våra kunder. Här finns det alltid möjlighet att lära sig om ny teknik och växa, både som individ och tillsammans med kollegorna.
   

  

**Ditt nya team**
 Teamet SAP Operations består av ett team på fjorton personer med bred teknisk kompetens som tillsammans ser till att Axfoods SAP landskap fungerar säkert och effektivt. Vi arbetar med moderna verktyg & teknik och ser fram emot att välkomna dig som kollega.
   

  

 Som Tech Platform Owner hos oss blir du också del av avdelningen Infrastructure Systems, som utöver teamet SAP Operations, består av specialister inom batchteknik och övervakning.
   

  

 Just nu är vi i avslutsfasen av transformation där vi gått från en on prem miljö till cloud och tillsammans med teamet får du vara med att leda den här förändringen.
   

  

**Ett ax-plock – Kollegornas favoriter på jobbet**
* SAP Operations är med och driver förändringen mot en ny och framtidssäkrad SAP Driftplattform
* Samarbete inom teamet och med andra team ger energi
* Vi jobbar nära våra kollegor i verksamheten, och ser till att både IT-kollegor och slutanvändare får bästa möjliga upplevelse av SAP-produkterna i vårt landskap


**Om rollen**
 Som Tech Platform Owner för SAP Operations ansvarar du för att leda och utveckla plattformen, både tekniskt och organisatoriskt. Du prioriterar teamets arbete, driver transformationen mot en ny referensarkitektur och säkerställer att våra lösningar är säkra, kostnadseffektiva och affärsnära.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer att inkludera:**
* Ansvara för teamets backlog och prioritera utifrån affärsvärde och teknisk genomförbarhet.
* Leda transformationen från tidigare on prem arkitektur till vår nya cloud baserade arkitektur.
* Säkerställa stabil och säker drift av SAP landskapet inom och mellan våra noder och plattformar.
* Samverka med Systemägare, Tech Platform Owners samt användare från andra områden inom Axfood IT.
* Arbeta nära arkitekt, Scrum-/Kanban Master och Tech Lead för att lyfta teamet.
* Inspirera, strukturera och skapa förutsättningar för utveckling och samarbete.
* Skapa relationer och arbeta tvärfunktionellt med andra ansvariga och team


**Din nya chef har ordet**
 ”Tillsammans är vi starkare! Samarbete inom teamet och med övriga team är det som gör skillnad om det blir framgång eller inte” – Sam Åberg, Tech Platform Area Manager
   

  

**Stämmer detta in på dig?**
 Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av att leda tekniska team och driva förändring inom cloud. Du gillar att skapa ordning, prioritera och få människor att växa. Du behöver vara trygg i att leda i en agil miljö med tydlig kommunikation. Du har fingertoppskänsla i samarbetet både i teamet och med övriga organisationen.
   

  

**Vi ser det som meriterande om du också har:**
* Erfarenhet av drift och leverans av SAP system.
* God kunskap om cloud-native lösningar.
* Förmåga att säkerställa hög plattformsprestanda och smidig användning.
* Erfarenhet av att leda team där automation och Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible) är det normala arbetssättet.
* Erfarenhet av drift i moln (Driftssäkerhet, monitorering, FinOps, säkerhet)


**Ansökningsprocessen**
**Ansökan till denna roll ligger ute över sommaren, men ansökningar kommer bahandlas först i augusti. Du är välkommen med din ansökan redan nu!**
 Vi vill att det ska vara enkelt att söka jobb hos oss. Om du inte har ett CV redo räcker det med att du länkar en väl ifylld LinkedIn-profil. I din ansökan får du svara på de frågor vi behöver svar på i den här delen av processen, och personligt brev är därför inte obligatoriskt. Vår rekryteringsprocess innehåller tester, intervjuer, bakgrundskontroll och ID-kontroll. Observera att vi inte under denna rekryteringsprocess kommer be dig logga in med ditt Bank-id eller scanna någon QR-kod, men vi kommer be dig styrka din identitet i processens slutskede.
   

  

 Vid frågor om rekrytering på Axfood, kontakta Medarbetarsupporten eller läs mer: Så går det till när du söker jobb inom Axfood. Om du har frågor om tjänsten, kontakta elisabeth.wehlin@axfood.se. Av GDPR-skäl behandlar vi inga ansökningar eller annan personlig information via mail.
   

  

 Läs mer och se filmer om Axfood som arbetsgivare här: Din karriärväg hos Axfood | Utveckling och möjligheter | Axfood

title: Driven ekonomiassistent (25%)
description:
🌍
 **Om Indivd**
 På Indivd hjälper vi några av världens mest välkända retail kedjor, gallerior och flygplatser att förstå hur människor rör sig i deras fysiska miljöer. Med hjälp av AI omvandlar vi anonymiserad besöksdata till insikter som hjälper våra kunder att fatta smartare affärsbeslut, från bemanning och butikslayout till marknadsföring och kundupplevelse.
   

  

 Vår teknik används idag av kunder som H&M, IKEA, Qatar Airways över 4 kontinenter och vi befinner oss i en spännande tillväxtfas där nya marknader, kunder och möjligheter öppnas varje dag.
   

  

 📝
 **Rollen**
 Du arbetar nära grundarteamet och får ett eget ansvarsområde från start. Det här är ingen roll i bakgrunden. Du hanterar fakturering, betalningsuppföljning och avstämningar av kundportföljen över flera marknader och valutor.
   

  

 Omfattning 25 till 30 procent i veckan, med helt flexibla arbetstider. Du jobbar på distans, med möjlighet att sitta på kontoret i Stockholm.
   

  

 🔧
 **Vad kommer du att göra?**
* Skicka och följa upp kundfakturor för vår internationella kundportfölj
* Följa upp utestående betalningar och säkerställa en välskött kundreskontra
* Genomföra kund- och leverantörsavstämningar samt identifiera och följa upp eventuella avvikelser
* Arbeta i Fortnox och stötta den löpande bokföringen
* Samarbeta med och vara kontaktperson mot vår externa redovisningsbyrå vid behov


 🎯
 **Vad vi letar efter**
* Du studerar ekonomi/finans/juridik, alternativt har motsvarande erfarenhet
* Du trivs i en startupmiljö där tempot är högt
* Du är strukturerad, noggrann och känner dig trygg med att arbeta självständigt
* Du har svenska som modersmål och kommunicerar obehindrat på engelska
* Du är nyfiken på hur ett snabbväxande AI- och SaaS-bolag fungerar i praktiken och vill vara med på resan.


 💜
 **💜 Därför kommer du att trivas på Indivd**
* Arbeta direkt med grundarna, inte flera chefsled bort
* Få ett verkligt ansvar från dag ett, inte bara administrativa uppgifter
* Arbeta helt på distans med flexibla arbetstider där du själv styr din vardag
* Få en unik inblick i hur ekonomin fungerar i ett snabbväxande internationellt AI- och SaaS-bolag


 📩
 **Ansök idag**

title: Senior Deployment Strategist, Professional Services
description:
**Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.**
 Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.
   

  

 Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.
   

  

 As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.
   

  

 We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.
   

  

 Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Planet’s Professional Services team helps our Defense and Intelligence (D&I) customers adopt the Planet Platform and rapidly realize return on investment in Planet data and analytics. The Professional Services Global D&I team is looking for a Solutions Architect responsible for the implementation, integration, and analysis of new software and data products that extend the Planet Platform and deliver new capabilities to our strategic D&I customers.
   

  

 In this role, you will be embedded with our customer to train users on Planet products, translate requirements, design workflows, and communicate with Planet’s product team on mission critical capabilities. Your work will be highly visible, directly impacting customers' ability to quickly access Planet's unprecedented datasets, as well as streamline the internal processes for adding new customers to our platform.
   

  

 This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our Stockholm, Sweden office 3 days per week.
   

  

**Impact You’ll Own**
* Understand clients’ needs and deliver tailored solutions
* Break down ambiguous, high-stakes problems into clearly defined, solvable components
* Leverage analytical skills to deliver products to our D&I customers and support mission requirements
* Design workflows, defining data models, configuring integrations, and helping define tooling that aligns to the customer’s mission.
* Train users, iterate with them in real time, and ensure that Planet solutions become mission-critical to daily operations.
* Translate operational requirements into actionable product feedback for Planet’s engineering teams.
* Communicate technical capabilities back to customers in clear, mission-focused language.
* Rapidly prototype, test, and adjust workflows to match real operational needs.
* Map high-level strategy with decision makers by sitting next to analysts to debug workflows.
* Collaborate with customer teams to understand their workflows, pain points, and mission priorities.


**What You Bring**
* 6+ years of experience in supporting D&I customers, with specialized technical experience in the general areas of systems integration
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software, Systems Engineering, or similar
* Fluent in Golang, Python, Postgresql, & React or related technologies
* Experience with cloud deployments; GCP, AWS, Azure
* Solid problem-solving and systems thinking skills
* Exceptional communication and stakeholder management
* Ability to quickly learn new technical domains and tools
* Comfortable operating in ambiguous, dynamic environments
* Fluency in translating between technical and non-technical audiences
* Experience with solving complex problems through analytical skills
* Experience scaling and maintaining production systems


**What Makes You Stand Out**
* Experience working with defense and intelligence organizations in the UAE
* Systems integrations experience working with multi domain/multi-level security architectures, particularly the Defence and Intelligence
* Working knowledge of JavaScript and its mapping libraries (OpenLayers, Leaflet, ArcGIS API for JavaScript), Google Earth Engine and its SDK
* Experience with RDBMS (PostgreSQL, SQL) and distributed databases (NoSQL)
* Experience in geopolitics and data science
* Ability to pick up new technologies quickly
* Experience with building analytical products from complex datasets
* Experience with geospatial technologies and data
* GEOINT experience, imagery exploitation and analysis
* Fluency in multiple languages


**Application Deadline**
 September 18, 2026 at 11:59p PT
   

  

**Export Control Requirements**
*Planet Labs Germany GmbH is authorized to receive U.S. export-controlled information at Planet under an export license issued by the Bureau of Industry and Security. This position requires access to export-controlled information, and as such, employment (or hiring of a contractor) is contingent upon the candidate’s ability to access all applicable export-controlled information without additional export licensing being required by the Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the Directorate of Defense Trade Controls.*
**Benefits While Working At Planet**
*These offerings are dependent on employment type and geographical location, based upon applicable law or company policy.*
* Paid time off including vacation, holidays and company-wide days off
* Employee Wellness Program
* Home Office Reimbursement
* Monthly Phone and Internet Reimbursement
* Tuition Reimbursement and access to LinkedIn Learning
* Equity
* Volunteering Paid Time Off


**San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance**
 Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.
   

  

**Why We Care So Much About Belonging.**
 We’re dedicated to helping the whole Planet, and to do that we must strive to represent all of it within each of our offices and on all of our teams. That’s why Planet is guided by an ultimate north star of Belonging—dreaming big as we approach our ongoing work. If this job intrigues you, but you’re thinking you might not have all the qualifications, please... do apply! At Planet, we are looking for well-rounded people from around the world who can contribute to more ways than just what is listed in this job description. We don’t just fill positions, we aspire to fulfill people’s careers, most excited about folks who are motivated by our underlying humanitarian efforts. We are a few orbits around the sun before we get to where we want to be, so we hope you’re excited to come along for the ride.
   

  

**EEO Statement**
 Planet is committed to building a community where everyone belongs and we invite people from all backgrounds to apply. Planet is an equal opportunity employer, and committed to providing employment opportunities regardless of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding, age, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification, in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Know Your Rights.
   

  

**Accommodations**
 Planet is an inclusive community and we know that everyone has their own needs. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during the hiring process, please reach out to accommodations@planet.com or contact your recruiter with your request. Your message will be confidential and we will be happy to assist you.
   

  

**Privacy Policy** 
 : By clicking "Apply Now" at the top of this job posting, I acknowledge that I have read the Planet Data Privacy Notice for California Staff Members and Applicants, and hereby consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of my personal information as described therein.
   

  

**Privacy Policy (European Applicants):** 
 By clicking "Apply Now" at the top of this job posting, I acknowledge that I have read the Candidate Privacy Notice GDPR Planet Labs Europe, and hereby consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of my personal information as described therein.
   

  

**AI in Our Interviewing Process** 
 : Planet is committed to providing an exceptional interview experience for all candidates. We currently use Metaview to better focus on candidates and less on trying to capture notes. As such, with the candidate's consent, select interviews may be recorded and include a "Planet AI Notetaker" for transcription and summarization purposes. Should an interview involve use of AI interview technologies, the candidate will receive notification and have the ability to opt out both in advance and/or real-time. Opting out will not affect one's candidacy.
   

  

**Candidate AI Policy** 
 : Planet embraces Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools, and we encourage its responsible use. We understand that candidates may use various resources, including AI tools, to
 *prepare* 
 for interviews and assessments. However,
 *during any live interview stage or when actively completing assessments for this position, the use of AI tools—e.g. Large Language Models (LLMs), deep fake technology, etc.—is strictly prohibited unless explicitly prompted by an interviewer or assessment instructions* 
 . If you are unsure about acceptable use, please contact your recruiter for clarification. If an AI tool or similar technology is desired as an accommodation, please contact accommodations@planet.com with your request for assistance. Your message will be confidential, and we will be happy to assist you. Violation of this policy may result in disqualification of your application.

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Subscriptions
description:
The Subscriptions mission helps people discover the right Spotify experience for them and supports the entire journey from free to paid. Our teams build products and services that make it easier for listeners around the world to understand the value of Spotify Premium while enabling sustainable growth for the business.
   

  

 The Markets & Subscriptions team oversees the entire user journey on Spotify and ensures we engage with people in innovative ways, every step of the way. Our team grows Spotify’s audience by finding future listeners around the world and delivering the right value to them, at the right time. With research, product development, product design, engineering, and marketing all collaborating in one organization, we’re able to quickly create meaningful features and services for millions of people around the world, resulting in joyful, long-lasting relationships with Spotify.
   

  

 We are looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join our cross-functional team of data scientists and mobile, web, and backend engineers to build the services that power Spotify's free-to-paid experiences.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Collaborate with a cross-functional engineering team to build and evolve Spotify's subscription conversion experiences.
* Design and develop scalable backend services and APIs using Java, gRPC, and GCP.
* Partner with teams across the Subscriptions mission to support new products, plans, and global launches.
* Build resilient backend systems that support millions of users across web and mobile.
* Work closely with product managers and engineers to deliver technical solutions that solve meaningful customer and business problems.
* Lead technical initiatives while mentoring teammates and raising engineering quality across the team.
* Champion engineering best practices, operational excellence, and continuous improvement.


 Who You Are
   

  

* You thrive in collaborative environments and enjoy solving complex technical problems with others.
* You have 5+ years of experience building backend systems at scale.
* You have experience with Java, gRPC, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
* You know how to design APIs and backend services consumed by multiple client platforms.
* You care about writing maintainable, high-quality, production-ready software.
* You have experience working with internal stakeholders to build reusable platforms and services.
* Experience with subscription, conversion, commerce, or merchandising systems is a plus.


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London, England or Stockholm, Sweden.
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Backend Developer - Java & GCP
description:
This team own the systems that define products, manage entitlements, enforce consumption quotas, and handle fulfillment for every subscription and add-on the company sells. We're looking for a backend contractor to work through our tech excellence backlog: deprecations, migrations, test improvements, and cleanup tasks that are well-scoped and don't require deep domain knowledge. You'll be guided by senior engineers who set direction and review your work.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Deprecate and remove legacy code, endpoints, and unused features
* Migrate services from legacy protocols and APIs to current standards
* Improve test reliability and coverage
* Clean up protobuf definitions and resolve naming inconsistencies across the monorepo
* Decommission old data pipelines and verify replacement infrastructure
* Address company wide engineering compliance items (dependency upgrades, framework migrations)
* Add documentation and logging where gaps exist


**Who You Are**
* 5+ years backend development in Java
* Comfortable reading unfamiliar codebases and making bounded changes without full system context
* Experience with gRPC and Protocol Buffers
* Experience with microservice architectures and service-to-service communication
* Familiar with Kubernetes and GCP (or equivalent cloud platform)
* Comfortable with CI/CD pipelines and monorepo workflows
* Self-directed: takes a scoped ticket, asks clarifying questions, delivers without close supervision
* Documents what you find and what you change


 Nice to have:
   

  

* Experience with data pipelines (dbt, BigQuery, dbeam)
* Familiarity with event-driven architectures (Pub/Sub)


 Previous experience with Backstage, monorepo, GCP infrastructure is a strong plus.
   

  

 Location: Stockholm Sweden.
   

  

 Period: 2026-08-10 to 2027-02-09

title: Senior Systemintegratör – IBM ACE
description:
Vi söker nu en erfaren Systemintegratör till ett långsiktigt uppdrag hos vår kund i Stockholm. Kunden ansvarar för organisationens integrationsplattform och behöver förstärka sitt team med senior kompetens inom integrationsutveckling, med fokus på IBM ACE.
 



  





 Som konsult på Eccera får du en trygg anställning och samlar på dig värdefull arbetslivserfarenhet på uppdrag hos våra kunder. Din konsultchef stöttar dig i din utveckling där du hela tiden fyller på med nya erfarenheter och kunskaper. En utmärkt möjlighet att göra karriär.
 



  





**Om uppdraget** 




 Du kommer att ingå i kundens integrationsgrupp som ansvarar för en verksamhetskritisk integrationsplattform. Integrationerna är ofta komplexa och verksamhetsnära, vilket ställer höga krav på teknisk kompetens, samarbetsförmåga och förståelse för större organisationers struktur och arbetssätt.
 



  





 Arbetet innebär att:
 


* Utveckla och förvalta integrationer i IBM ACE
* Arbeta i nära samarbete med verksamhet, arkitekter och andra utvecklare
* Delta i design, implementation och vidareutveckling av integrationslösningar
* Bidra till att driva integrationsarbetet framåt
* Successivt ta ett större ansvar inom integrationsområdet



 Uppdraget är långsiktigt och ger möjlighet att växa i ansvar över tid.
 



  





**Din profil** 




 Vi söker dig som är analytisk, lösningsorienterad och trivs med att arbeta i komplexa miljöer. Du är van att samarbeta i team, kommunicerar tydligt och har förmåga att ta ansvar och driva arbete framåt.
 



  





 Krav:
 


* Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka sig i tal och skrift på svenska
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av integrationsutveckling med IBM ACE
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet i rollen som Systemintegratör



  





 Meriterande kompetens:
 


* Erfarenhet av arbete inom offentlig sektor
* Erfarenhet av liknande Dynamics-uppdrag inom större organisationer
* Minst 3 års dokumenterad erfarenhet (de senaste 5 åren) av arbete med Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations
* Erfarenhet av liknande Dynamics-uppdrag
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom rättsväsendet



  





 Uppdraget kan komma att omfattas av säkerhetsskydd. Säkerhetsprövning och säkerhetsklassning kommer att genomföras innan uppdragsstart. Svenskt medborgarskap kan komma att krävas.
 



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 På Eccera får du de verktyg du behöver för att nå dina mål. Vi erbjuder alla våra konsulter kvalificerade utbildningar och certifieringar inom den senaste tekniken. Vi sätter stort värde på att våra medarbetare hela tiden ska fortsätta att utvecklas. Därför erbjuder vi dig möjligheten att lägga upp en individuell utbildningsplan, där vi bekostar såväl studiematerial som själva certifieringarna. Under din utveckling kommer vi matcha din kompetens mot rätt uppdrag i takt med att du avancerar.

title: Senior Account Executive - Defense (Public & Industry)
description:
Att välja Capgemini innebär att välja ett företag där du får möjlighet att forma din karriär på det sätt du själv önskar, där du stöttas och inspireras av en samarbetsinriktad gemenskap av kollegor över hela världen, och där du får möjlighet att tänka om kring vad som är möjligt. Gå med i vårt team och hjälp världens ledande organisationer att frigöra teknikens värde och bygga en mer hållbar och inkluderande värld.
   

  

 Vill du vara med och forma framtidens försvarsförmåga genom teknik och digital innovation?
   

  

 Capgemini är en global ledare inom transformation av försvars- och säkerhetssektorn. Vi stödjer myndigheter och försvarsindustri med avancerade lösningar inom digitalisering, AI, data, cyber, systemintegration och säkra molnplattformar. I takt med en snabbt förändrad omvärld ökar behovet av robusta, interoperabla och framtidssäkra lösningar – här spelar vi en nyckelroll.
   

  

 Nu stärker vi vårt team i Sverige med en Senior Account Executive inom Defense med tydligt fokus på tillväxt och ny affär.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 I rollen som Account Executive har du ett tydligt fokus på att både skapa nya affärer och utveckla befintliga kundrelationer till långsiktiga partnerskap. Du arbetar mot kunder inom offentlig försvarssektor och försvarsindustri, där du driver affärer i komplexa miljöer med höga krav på säkerhet, kvalitet och förtroende.
   

  

 Detta är en roll med stort strategiskt inflytande, där du inte bara identifierar möjligheter – du formar, leder och driver större transformationer från idé till genomförande. Du arbetar nära kunden för att förstå deras verksamhet och behov på djupet, och positionerar Capgemini som en end-to-end partner inom digital transformation.
   

  

 Du ansvarar för hela säljcykeln, från proaktiv bearbetning och kvalificering av affärsmöjligheter till att leda större affärer i mål, ofta i samarbete med multidisciplinära team från vår globala organisation.
   

  

 Du kommer att:
   

  

* Driva både nykundsbearbetning och utveckling av befintliga kunder
* Identifiera, forma och driva större transformationsaffärer
* Bygga långsiktiga, förtroendebaserade relationer och positionera Capgemini som strategisk partner
* Leda hela affärsprocessen – från idé, kvalificering och lösningsdesign till förhandling och avslut
* Arbeta nära kundens ledning för att förstå strategiska prioriteringar och koppla dessa till Capgeminis erbjudande
* Koordinera och leda interna team för att leverera sammanhängande end-to-end lösningar
* Utveckla och expandera affärer inom områden som AI, data, cloud, cyber och systemintegration


**Din profil**
 Vi söker en affärsdriven och resultatorienterad person med tydlig hunter-mentalitet:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av försvarssektorn
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av komplex försäljning (IT- och konsulttjänster)
* Stark förmåga att bygga förtroende och utveckla relationer
* Stark förmåga att skapa nya affärer och bevisad erfarenhet av att utveckla befintliga affärer över tid
* Erfarenhet av att driva större komplexa affärer och transformationsprogram
* Erfarenhet av offentliga upphandlingar (LOU) är meriterande
* Förståelse för säkerhetsklassade miljöer och regulatoriska krav
* Befintligt nätverk inom försvarssektorn är mycket meriterande
* Flytande svenska och engelska


**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas**
* Möjlighet att arbeta med några av Sveriges mest strategiska organisationer inom försvar och säkerhet
* Tillgång till Capgeminis globala Defense-nätverk och expertis
* Ett brett erbjudande inom teknik och affärstransformation – från rådgivning till implementation
* Stor affärspotential och möjlighet att påverka din egen portfölj
* Konkurrenskraftiga villkor och kollektivavtal


**Övrigt**
*Tjänsten kräver godkänd säkerhetsprövning.*
**Ansökan**
*Ansökningar kommer börja hanteras med start vecka 33 och intervjuer sker löpande. Observera att identitets- och bakgrundskontroller kommer genomföras som en del av rekryteringsprocessen.*
*För frågor om rollen kontakta Jessica Fogelsjöö, jessica.fogelsjoo@capgemini.com.*
**Om Capgemini**
 Capgemini är en AI-driven global partner för affärs- och tekniktransformation som levererar konkret verksamhetsnytta. Vi formar framtiden för organisationer och förverkligar den med hjälp av AI, teknik och människor. Med vår starka historia på nästan 60 år är vi en ansvarsfull och mångsidig koncern med 420 000 medarbetare i över 50 länder. Genom vår expertis inom strategi, teknik, design, ingenjörstjänster och affärsverksamhet levererar vi heltäckande tjänster och lösningar, baserade på vår djupa branschkunskap och vårt omfattande partnernätverk. Koncernen rapporterade globala intäkter på 22,1 miljarder euro för 2024.
   

  

**Make it real** 
 | www.capgemini.com

title: Functional consultant Dynamics 365 CE Stockholm
description:
**Jobbeskrivning**
 Med över 50 000 experter globalt och som certifierad och managerad Microsoft Cloud Partner på högsta nivån, har vi kompetensen och specialiseringar att leverera hållbara, smarta lösningar som gör verklig skillnad. På Sopra Steria förenar vi teknik och syfte för att driva digital transformation med Azure OpenAI, Copilot, BI, Dynamics 365 och Power Platform. Tillsammans med våra kunder förenklar vi processer, stärker kundrelationer och skapar värde för både verksamhet och samhälle. Genom att tillämpa AI där det ger störst effekt hjälper vi organisationer att ta nästa steg i sin digitala resa.
   

  

 Rollen som funktionell konsult är bred och här jobbar du nära verksamheten och dina arbetsuppgifter kan till exempel inkludera anpassningar av systemet, framtagande av lösningsförslag, konfiguration, eller att på andra sätt hjälpa verksamheter framåt. Ofta arbetar vi också i en helhetsleverans tillsammans med kollegor från oss på Sopra Steria.
   

  

**Om Dig**
 Du är van att arbeta inom Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE och trivs med att skapa värde mot kund. Du har en förmåga att förstå företagsbehov och gillar att arbeta verksamhetsnära.
   

  

* Minst 4 års erfarenhet inom Dynamics 365 CE
* Kunna konfigurera i Dynamics 365 CRM (skapa tabeller, vyer, formulär etc.)
* Arbetat med tjänster inom Power Platform (low-code lösningar baserat på Power Apps och Power Automate)
* Erfarenhet och/eller intresse av att arbeta med Copilot Studio
* God kommunikativ förmåga på svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


**Att jobba på Sopra Steria**
 Hos oss får du ett stabilt bolag med stora kunder och resurser, i kombination med entreprenörsanda och stark teamkänsla. Vi erbjuder utmanande uppdrag där du kan bidra tekniskt för att göra skillnad i samhället. Med kollektivavtal, marknadsmässiga löner, flexibilitet och en kultur präglad av kunskapsdelning och samarbete vill vi ge dig riktigt bra förutsättningar för att lyckas. Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss här.
   

  

**Några av våra förmåner:**
* 5 000 kr i friskvård samt hälsostöd för dig och din familj
* 30 dagars semester samt två extra lediga dagar per år
* Certifieringsbonus och aktiv kompetensutveckling
* Initiativ för träning och gemenskap
* Stark föräldralön, tjänstepension och försäkringar
* Bidrag till hemmakontor och arbetsutrustning


**Ansök nu – vi ser fram emot att träffa dig!**
 På Sopra Steria ställer vi höga krav på både kompetens och säkerhetsmedvetenhet hos våra medarbetare. Innan en eventuell anställning kommer du därför att genomgå en bakgrundskontroll, och vid intervju behöver du kunna uppvisa giltig legitimation.
   

  

 Rekryteringsprocessen består av tre intervjuer, personlighet- och logiktest, referenstagning och bakgrundskontroll. Denna kombination av urvalsmetoder ger oss en bredare och mer rättvis bild av dig som kandidat, samtidigt som det säkerställer att både dina tekniska och personliga styrkor kommer till sin rätt. För dig som kandidat innebär det en möjlighet att visa din kompetens och potential från flera perspektiv.
   

  

 Om du har frågor om tjänsten eller oss som bolag är du välkommen att kontakta Theresia Wiclander, theresia.wiclander@soprasteria.com

title: Systems Engineer Test Centre
description:
Brand: Kalmar
Team: R&D & Engineering
Job Location: Ljungby, SWE
Information at a Glance



Systems Engineer Test Centre
*At Kalmar, we are moving goods in critical supply chains around the world. As the forerunner in sustainable material handling equipment and services, we set the industry benchmark for a zero-emission future. We are driven by teamwork, innovation, and taking ownership to deliver results. With our global reach and local expertise, our success starts with a strong focus on the customer, ensuring their needs guide our daily work. We are dedicated to respecting others and fostering an inclusive culture where all voices are heard, empowering us to succeed together. With us, your work has an impact. Your work moves the world!*


**Join us and take your career to the next level**
--------------------------------------------------

  

We’re expanding our testing operations in Ljungby and are looking for a new **Systems Engineer Test Centre** to join us at our brand-new Test Centre within the Innovation Centre. In this role, you’ll help shape how we validate the next generation of CB Division’s products, utilizing our latest facilities to turn R&D concepts into proven reality. In this position, you will take a leading technical role in test execution focusing on designing, developing, and performing product tests that ensure our machines meet customer expectations, regulatory requirements, and our own ambitious business goals. As a key technical specialist in our newly established Test Centre, you will play a central role in defining test methods, tools, and software frameworks that will set the standard for how we verify product performance going forward.


The role is based in Ljungby, Sweden and you will report to the Product Test Manager.

  

**What are your key responsibilities:**
---------------------------------------

  

* **Method Development:** Identify and create new test methods for all CB machines at our Test Centre.
* **System Programming:** Take ownership of setting up programs for our test equipment software and managing the data results.
* **Machine Evaluation:** Challenge the capabilities of our products in our Test Centre to find potential improvements.
* **Operational Excellence:** Prepare, perform, and report on tests according to schedules and routines, ensuring any deviations are identified and addressed early.
* **Stakeholder Collaboration:** Work closely with other R&D functions to plan activities and define the future ways of working within the Test Centre.
**What we offer you:**
----------------------

  

*"At Kalmar, safety isn’t just a standard — it’s a commitment built into every product, process, and decision."*

  

* **Flexibility that works** – hybrid work and a balance between work and leisure.
* **Unlimited development opportunities** – education and career growth.
* **Innovative technology** – work with industry-leading equipment and solutions.
* **Meaningful work** – contribute to sustainable development and global trade.
* **Strong company culture** – become part of Kalmar's values and community.
* **Technical challenge** – every day brings new problems to solve.

**Become part of our global network** of 5,200 professionals across 120+ countries, where your work drives innovation, sustainability, and meaningful impact around the world.


**What you bring to the role:**
-------------------------------

  

* **Education:** A university degree in Engineering or equivalent knowledge through professional experience.
* **Technical Expertise:** Solid experience in product testing and knowledge of rubber-tired vehicles (CB products or similar).
* **Software Skills:** A strong background in Software Programming and familiarity with engineering design tools.
* **Analytical Mindset:** The ability to work structurally, plan effectively, and solve complex technical problems under a schedule.
* **Attitude:** A positive, "can-do" attitude with a business-driven focus on quality and performance.
* **Language skills:** Fluent in both Swedish and English (written and spoken) is essential for interacting with stakeholders and reporting outcomes.
**Ready to elevate your career? Apply today!**
----------------------------------------------

  

Take the next step in your career journey with Kalmar! Please submit your CV in English.

  

Closing Date: 5/7/2026

  

**About Kalmar**
  

*Kalmar (Nasdaq Helsinki: KALMAR) is moving goods in critical supply chains around the world, with the vision to be the forerunner in sustainable material handling equipment and services. The company offers a wide range of industry shaping heavy material handling equipment and services to ports and terminals, distribution centres, manufacturing and heavy logistics. Headquartered in Helsinki, Finland, Kalmar operates globally in over 120 countries and employs approximately 5,200 people. In 2024, the company's sales totalled approximately EUR 1.7 billion.*
  

*www.kalmarglobal.com*
company_description: Kalmar is the industry forerunner in terminal automation and in energy efficient container handling, with one in four container movements around the globe being handled by a Kalmar solution.

title: Tech Customer Support Administrator
description:
Job Description
  
At Sinch, we are a global leader in providing cutting-edge communication platforms, enabling businesses to connect with any mobile phone on the planet in seconds, or less. Our platform supports mobile SMS, rich messaging, voice, and video communication solutions for some of the biggest companies in the world. We are expanding our support team looking for a passionate and driven Tech Customer Support Administrator II to join us in the Sweden.
  
  

**What We Do:**  

Our support team plays a crucial role in ensuring our customers receive the best possible experience while using our products. Operating 24/7, we tackle challenges and resolve customer issues from around the globe, helping customers across industries. As we expand, we are building a follow-the-sun support structure to address customer tickets efficiently and effectively.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
  

**As Support Engineer, you will:**  

Provide high-quality technical support to operators and enterprises worldwide.
  
Analyze customer messaging traffic trends and patterns in our production environment.
  
Perform deep triage and troubleshooting of technical issues across both software application and network layers.
  
Support Sinch colleagues, including the sales team, during customer integration, onboarding, and post-deployment phases.
  
Collaborate directly with customers in various technical areas, while working with state-of-the-art communication technologies, including rich messaging and collaborations with leading suppliers like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta.
  
  

Who Are You?
  
This is a challenging and dynamic role that requires a strong desire for learning and self-improvement. You should thrive in a fast-paced, ever-evolving environment and view each problem as an opportunity to learn and grow. You will be part of a global team where your ideas will be heard and valued!
  
  

**To be successful in this role, you should possess:**  

Experience working directly with global enterprise customers in a technical capacity.
  
Strong understanding of TCP/IP protocol suite and web protocols.
  
Proficiency in using service ticketing systems such as Zendesk, Atlassian Jira Service Management, etc.
  
Fluency in English (both written and spoken) for effective communication with global teams and customers.
  
  

**Big Plus! To stand out, you should ideally have:**  

A degree in Computer Science or a relevant technical field, or other formal IT/technical qualifications.
  
Basic knowledge of UNIX/Linux systems, HTTP, SMPP, Rest, SQL, Postman.
  
Background in telecommunications or wireless communication.
  
Familiarity with mobile communications standards like GSM and 4G.
  
Knowledge of the SS7 protocol suite and SIGTRAN.
  
  

The role requires flexibility to work on rotating shifts covering EMEA and US regions.
  
Why Sinch? At Sinch, you'll have the chance to work with some of the most advanced communication technologies in the world. This role will give you a chance to grow technically and professionally as you interact with both customers and cutting-edge technologies. You will have the support of a collaborative, forward-thinking team that values innovation, continuous learning, and personal development.
  
Our Commitment
  
We strive to cultivate a motivated and hardworking team in a welcoming and diverse workplace. Our core values—Dream Big, Win Together, Keep It Simple, and Make It Happen—form the basis for a culture that encourages innovative thinking and drives improved business outcomes through diverse perspectives and skills.
  
The path to success is always upward, and opportunities are closer than you think. It’s time to chase answers, tackle challenges, and follow your dreams.
  
Ready to join us on this journey? Apply now!
  
If this role isn’t what you’re looking for, please consider other open roles on our career page: Sinch Careers

title: Product Engineer
description:
*Want to work with hardware products used worldwide and help ensure they remain reliable, manufacturable, and competitive throughout their lifecycle? If you enjoy combining technical problem-solving with real product impact, this could be your next step.*


HMS stands for Hardware Meets Software and we work to make the world more connected, productive, and sustainable. Our industrial communication products enable millions of machines such as robots, drives, and air-conditioning systems to connect with software and information systems, supporting the transition toward more energy-efficient and sustainable industries.

**What you'll do**
------------------


We are looking for a Product Engineer to join our Hardware team in the Industrial Network Technology (INT) Division in Halmstad. In this role, you will ensure that our hardware products remain reliable, cost-efficient, and manufacturable throughout their lifecycle. You will work with products that evolve as components change, technologies develop, and new requirements emerge. You will collaborate closely with teams across Hardware, Product Management, Production Quality, Planning, and Global Sourcing, contributing to technical improvements and long-term product performance. At HMS, you will deepen your expertise in electronics and product lifecycle management while working with products used globally in industrial communication and automation.

*Your key responsibilities will include:*

* Managing component lifecycle challenges, including second sourcing and obsolete components
* Driving Design for Manufacturing (DFM) in product updates and improvements
* Leading cost-reduction and technical improvement initiatives
* Supporting quality investigations and resolving technical product issues
* Contributing to production test specifications and manufacturing readiness
* Maintaining technical documentation
* Collaborating with engineering, sourcing, production, and quality teams
* Ensuring long-term product competitiveness and manufacturability

*Tools & engineering environment:*  

We use Jira for project and issue tracking, and Altium Designer and OrCAD for electronics design and PCB development. You will work closely with production and quality teams using structured processes to manage component changes and manufacturing readiness.

**About you**
-------------


You enjoy understanding how products work and finding ways to improve them. You are curious and structured, and motivated by working with a broad range of technical challenges throughout a product’s lifecycle. You collaborate well with different functions and stakeholders and communicate clearly across teams and locations. You take ownership of your work, pay attention to detail, and combine technical problem-solving with practical business considerations.

*To succeed in this role, we believe you have:*

* A university degree in electronics, mechatronics, mechanical engineering, software engineering, or a related technical field
* Experience in electronics development and/or electronics maintenance
* A structured way of working with strong attention to detail
* Strong communication skills in English
* Ambition and drive to continue developing both technically and professionally

*Nice to have:*

* Knowledge of component lifecycle management and second sourcing
* Familiarity with Design for Manufacturing (DFM)
* Exposure to electronics production and/or production transfers
* Experience with cost-reduction activities
* Previous work in customer-facing roles
* Experience from cross-functional or international environments
* Swedish language skills

**About us**
------------


At HMS, we put Heart, Mind & Soul into everything we do. These core values not only make for a fitting abbreviation (HMS), but also describe how we work together and create success. We are an innovative growth company headquartered in Halmstad, Sweden, with approximately 1,100 employees in more than 20 countries. Through our global presence, we combine the opportunities of an international organisation with the close-knit and entrepreneurial spirit that has characterised HMS since the beginning. HMS is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an environment where everyone feels included, respected, and empowered.

  

Visit hms-networks.com if you want to learn more.

**Welcome with your application!**
----------------------------------


Please note that we are currently on summer holiday, which means feedback on applications may be delayed until the end of August.

title: Post-doctoral fellow in Particle- and Nuclear Physics
description:
Lund University was founded in 1666 and is repeatedly ranked among the world’s top universities. The University has around 46 000 students and 8 500 staff based in Lund, Helsingborg and Malmö. We are united in our efforts to understand, explain and improve our world and the human condition.

**Description of the workplace**
================================


This postdoc position will be part of the Lund LDMX group, LunDMX, embedded in the Division for Particle and Nuclear Physics at the Physics Department. The division, with about 60 employees, comprises a lively mixture of active research in theoretical and experimental particle and heavy ion physics as well as experimental nuclear physics. The experimental particle physics activities include research at the well-established LHC experiments ATLAS and ALICE as well as efforts to realise new, creative experiments like HIBEAM/NNBAR at ESS and – most relevant for this project – LDMX at SLAC (https://confluence.slac.stanford.edu/display/MME/Light+Dark+Matter+Experiment). We exploit synergies across these projects, and our contributions include detector development, trigger and DAQ, physics and performance analysis as well as software development and computing. The LunDMX group currently consists of a postdoc, three faculty members and three Ph.D. students, and an engineer. This postdoc position is an important addition to our group, it will inject additional momentum to our work and strengthen our impact on the overall LDMX project. This position is also to serve as a stepping stone for the successful candidate, with ample opportunity for career development, for which we strive to give all the support we can.  

  



**Project description**
=======================


The Light Dark Matter eXperiment is a fixed-target experiment planned at SLAC with the main purpose of searching for sub-GeV thermal-relic dark matter particles produced from an 8 GeV electron beam impinging on a thin target. The Lund LDMX group (LunDMX) contributes to physics analysis, detector development and performance studies as well as software development and computing. In addition, we are active in addressing sustainability questions related to developing a new particle physics experiment.


The overarching theme of this project is to get ready for LDMX data, and the work will be conducted in close connection with the international LDMX collaboration, in which Lund University is currently the only non-US participant. The main priority in this project will be to study the capabilities of LDMX to handle pile-up (multiple electrons hitting the target at the same time) in more detail. This will be based on simulation studies of potential backgrounds, detector performance, and physics sensitivities. One concrete example is the use of different codes for simulating critical backgrounds, comparing these predictions and developing methods for how to measure these backgrounds in data. The project also aims to explore to which extent machine learning methods can help with these tasks, e.g. object reconstruction and signal/background discrimination. This will be a focus in the project.


One exciting milestone for LDMX is the detector prototype system test with cosmic muons planned at SLAC in 2026. If the candidate is interested, there will be the opportunity to contribute to the analysis of this test data, mainly from the prototype of the hadronic calorimeter. Closely related to this, there is also the possibility to contribute to the characterisation of the HCal electronics with a test stand locally in Lund.


There is some flexibility in the research programme for this position, and details can be tailored to your specific interests and expertise. There will also be the opportunity to work with questions of climate sustainability in LDMX/HEP.


**Tasks and responsibilities**
==============================


At Lund University, a postdoctoral position shall provide the opportunity for scientific and pedagogical merit. There is primarily scope for merit in research, but also for some higher education pedagogical training. Teaching may also be included in the duties.


As a postdoc in the LunDMX group, you will mainly perform simulation-based studies of different aspects feeding into the physics potential of LDMX. This will require the use (and further development) of different simulation tools, mainly the GEANT4-based ldmx-sw framework (https://github.com/LDMX-Software/ldmx-sw ), written in C++, maintained on github and configured via python. You will work with .root files and explore different event generators as well as machine learning tools and algorithms. The position will also require you to contribute to the development of data-driven methods.


The nature of LDMX as an international project will require you to work collaboratively in an international research environment.  

Your main place of work will be at the Physics Department in Lund, but trips to SLAC/our collaborators will also be necessary.  

The position requires clear and thoughtful communication, both within the scientific community and with the public.  

You will contribute to the development and maintenance of the common LDMX software.  

You will be responsible for documentation and reporting of your research activities, maintaining detailed records of your work, preparing documentation for future users and following the LDMX requirements for contributing to documentation.  

You are expected to participate in mentorship and training of junior researchers, fostering a collaborative and knowledge-sharing environment.  

You will be given the opportunity to seek external research funding for travel or small projects, as well as to contribute to sustainability studies, if you wish.  

Your tasks will also include taking care of administration related to the duties outlined above.

**Qualifications**
==================

**Requirements for the position are:**

* Doctoral degree or equivalent foreign degree in Physics. The doctoral degree must have been obtained no later than the date on which the appointment decision is made.
* Ability to develop and conduct high-quality research.
* Teaching ability
* Prior experience with simulations and data analysis in particle physics or similar
* Strong programming skills in C++ and Python
* Prior experience in the usage, development and maintenance of common software
* Collaborative skills in order to work in an international collaboration.
* Very good oral and written proficiency in English
* Scholarly proficiency, for example demonstrated through published papers and/or the quality of your PhD dissertation.
* Preparedness for international travel

**Merits for the position are:**


Below follows a list of qualifications that should be read as “and/or”, not “and” – if you fulfil any of the listed requirements, it is a plus, but we do not expect you to tick all the boxes simultaneously.

* Applicants who graduated no more than three years before the closing date for applications
* Knowledgable about interactions in the borderland energy regime between nuclear and particle physics
* Experienced in developing algorithms such as particle identification, specific final state event selection, and object reconstruction
* Prior experience with machine learning tools
* Interest and prior experience in student supervision (bachelor, master, PhD)


The assessment of applicants will primarily be based on their scientific merits and potential as researchers. Particular emphasis will be placed on scientific ability within the subject area.

**What we offer**
=================


Lund University is a government agency, which means that you get special benefits, generous holidays and a favourable occupational pension.  

Lund University has generous support for parental leave and favourable offers for training and wellness.  

Find out more on the University website about working with us Work for us

**About the employment**
========================


The position is a fixed-term position for two years, 100% with a start date of October 1, 2026, or as agreed. Employment at Lund University is based in Sweden, so you are expected to be able to perform your work on site in Lund.  

The position is limited to two years in accordance with the collective agreement on fixed-term employment as a postdoctoral researcher (Arbetsgivarverket). A position as a postdoctoral researcher is intended as an initial step in your career and as an opportunity to further deepen and broaden your research expertise

**Other information**
=====================

If you have any questions, fell free to contact ruth.pottgen@fysik.lu.se.

**How to apply**
================


Applications are submitted via the university’s recruitment system, where you are also asked to answer a couple of questions. Applications shall be written in English and contain (as pdf):

* A cover letter motivating your interest in this position and how this position matches your qualifications
* A statement of your current and future research interests (up to two pages)
* A CV with copies of PhD and exam certificates. You may supply a written statement with their expected defence date.
* A list of up to 10 relevant publications, annotated with your contributions
* A list with contact information for three references
  

Letters of recommendation are not requested at this time
  

References will be contacted if a candidate is invited to interview
* Any other documents you wish to submit for review


Welcome to apply!


Within the Faculty of Science research and education is conducted within Astronomy, Biology, Physics, Geosciences, Chemistry, Mathematics, medical radiation physics, physical geography and Environmental Sciences. The Faculty of Science is organized into eight departments, gathered in the northern campus area in Lund. The Faculty of Science has approximately 1900 students, 330 PhD students and 730 employees.


The Department of Physics is one of Lund University's largest departments with approximately 400 employees. The department is shared between the Faculty of Science and Faculty of Engineering. At the department there are seven research divisions and a number of major research centra. The research at the department covers a wide range of modern physics.
  

www.fysik.lu.se/en/  




We kindly decline all sales and marketing contacts.

title: HPC Field Support Engineer
description:
**About Bull**

  



Bull is a story. One with a century of European innovation and a working environment where experts design powerful, sustainable, and sovereign digital solutions, enabling states and industries to retain full control over their data and their AI.

  



Bull is also thousands of engineers, researchers and passionate tech people shaping the future of high‑performance computing, AI, and quantum technologies.

  



Every day, our teams push the boundaries of what is technologically possible – from next‑generation HPC architectures to exascale supercomputers – supported by world‑class R&D, more than 1,600 patents, and unique end‑to‑end capabilities spanning hardware design, software engineering, data science and quantum research.

  



We are a people‑centric, innovation‑driven company, where collaboration spans Europe, the Americas and India. We share a common vision of a responsible and sustainable innovation that delivers concrete impact for our customers.

  



We are searching for an **HPC Field Support Engineer** to support a key customer on-site in **Linköping, Sweden**.

  


**Role description:**



In this role, you will work directly from the customer’s data centre, supporting the installation, maintenance, and day-to-day operation of advanced HPC/AI systems. You will act as the primary interface between the customer and backline support teams, ensuring smooth operations and efficient issue resolution.



You will gain exposure to the full lifecycle of HPC/AI environments, including hardware, system software, and infrastructure support, while developing hands-on technical expertise.



This is a great opportunity for candidates early in their careers to gain hands-on experience in hardware support, Linux systems, and HPC environments, while working directly with a key customer in a production setting.  



**Responsibilities:**


* Provide on-site technical support for HPC/AI systems in a customer data centre
* Perform hardware maintenance and parts replacement (servers, components, etc.)
* Deliver Level 1 system software support, primarily in Linux environments
* Troubleshoot hardware, OS, and basic network-related issues
* Act as the interface between the customer and Bull’s support teams
* Support system installations, upgrades, and lifecycle activities
* Participate periodically in HPC/AI deployments across Europe
* Ensure high system availability and contribute to continuous improvement of support processes

  



**Skills & Competencies:**


* Hands-on experience with computer hardware (build, repair, maintenance)
* Good working knowledge of Linux systems
* Basic shell scripting skills
* Understanding of networking fundamentals and network security
* Strong technical interest in computing infrastructure and engineering
* Strong communication skills, able to explain technical topics clearly
* Confident working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Good interpersonal skills and ability to build trust with customers
* Problem-solving mindset with curiosity and willingness to learn
* Well-organised with strong attention to detail

  



**Why apply?**



This is a great opportunity to develop your **hardware and system software engineering skills** while gaining hands-on experience working closely with customer technical teams to maintain high-end HPC/AI systems.



As part of a comprehensive technical training program, you will receive practical training in **system hardware maintenance, HPC cluster management software, and high-performance storage**, helping you build a strong foundation in high-performance computing environments.

  



**What We Offer:**


* Hands-on experience with cutting-edge HPC and AI systems
* Structured technical training (hardware, cluster management, high-performance storage)
* Exposure to the full HPC/AI lifecycle
* A dynamic and international working environment
* Opportunities for professional growth and long-term career development
* A collaborative and supportive team culture
* Access to a strong technical network to support your development in HPC engineering



#Bull

  





Here, your ideas, your curiosity and your technical excellence directly shape the next era of advanced computing - unlocking enterprise value, accelerating scientific progress and driving positive impact for society.

title: Rolling Stock Testing & Commissioning Engineers
description:
We are seeking an experienced **Rolling Stock Testing & Commissioning Engineers** to join our engineering team in Gothenburg. This role sits within a leading rail OEM working on the delivery of new rolling stock programmes for the European market. You will play a key role in ensuring vehicles are tested, validated, and commissioned in line with technical, contractual, and regulatory requirements.


This is an excellent opportunity for a hands‑on engineer who enjoys working across depots, test tracks, and customer sites in a technically challenging environment.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Execute and manage testing and commissioning activities for new rolling stock projects (static, dynamic, and on‑track testing).
* Prepare, review, and approve test procedures, test specifications, and commissioning documentation.
* Lead fault diagnosis, troubleshooting, and root‑cause analysis during testing and commissioning phases.
* Interface closely with design engineering, manufacturing, quality, safety, and project teams to resolve technical issues.
* Ensure compliance with European standards, TSIs, safety cases, and customer technical requirements.
* Support homologation, type approval, and final handover of vehicles to the customer.
* Liaise with customers, suppliers, and certification bodies during testing activities.
* Provide technical input to risk assessments, change management, and lessons‑learned processes.
 **Qualifications**

* Degree or equivalent qualification in Electrical, Mechanical, Systems, or Railway Engineering.
* **Minimum 5 years’ experience** in testing and commissioning of rolling stock within a **European train manufacturing (OEM) environment**.
* Strong knowledge of rolling stock subsystems (e.g. traction, TCMS, brakes, HVAC, doors, communications, or auxiliaries).
* Practical experience with fault finding on new build or refurbished trains.
* Good understanding of European rail standards, TSIs, EN norms, and safety processes.
* Ability to work independently on depot and site‑based activities.
* Willingness to travel occasionally within Sweden and Europe.

**Desirable**

* Experience working on metro, commuter, or mainline passenger vehicles.
* Familiarity with commissioning in Nordic or Scandinavian rail networks.
* Knowledge of vehicle software integration and TCMS testing.
* Experience supporting entry‑into‑service activities.

title: Full-Stack Engineer - Survey & Design
description:
*10% of Europe’s CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.*

*With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we’re making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.*

*Founded in 2022, and with 1200+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, and HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.*

***About the role***


As a Full-Stack Engineer in our Survey & Design team, you'll help build the products that support some of the most important steps in Aira's customer acquisition journey.
  

  

The team is responsible for the tools used to capture and manage information about our customers' homes, supporting everything from home surveys and technical assessments to installation design. These products help ensure that the right information is available throughout the process, enabling tailored and efficient clean energy solutions.
  

  

You'll join a small, autonomous product team and work closely with product managers, designers and engineers to solve real-world challenges and build scalable solutions that support Aira's continued growth across Europe.
  

As a Full-Stack Engineer, you'll play a key role in shaping the products and services that power these workflows. You'll work across the stack to build scalable solutions and great user experiences.

***In this role, you will***

* Design and build applications and services that support users across multiple European markets
* Develop scalable APIs and integrations with third-party systems
* Process and manage data collected throughout the Survey & Design journey
* Transform complex technical and geometric data into solutions that support our products and workflows
* Contribute to architectural decisions and the long-term evolution of the platform
* Collaborate closely with product managers, designers and end users to improve workflows, user experiences and product capabilities
* Explore new technologies and approaches that help solve real business problems

We're making home energy design smarter, simpler and more scalable. If you enjoy building great products and solving complex problems in a technically challenging domain, this role is for you.

***What you'll bring to the team***

* Around 5+ years of experience as a Software Engineer
* Experience building modern software applications across different parts of the stack
* Strong experience with Java and Spring Boot
* Experience designing and building APIs
* A solid understanding of databases, software architecture and distributed systems
* Experience working with microservices
* The ability to turn ideas into clean, testable and scalable software
* A proactive mindset and willingness to take ownership
* Strong collaboration and communication skills

We believe you'll thrive in this role if you're curious, analytical and enjoy understanding the real-world challenges behind the software you build. You don't need a background in engineering, but an interest in technical domains and complex problem-solving will help you succeed.


We're also looking for someone who enjoys learning new technologies and is curious about how emerging technologies, including AI, can create value for both products and ways of working.

*Our tech stack:*


Java, Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, Kafka, gRPC, Kotlin, Python, React, TypeScript, Next.js, Kubernetes and Docker.

***What is in it for you?***

* Join a mission-driven company accelerating the transition to clean energy across Europe
* Work on products that have a direct impact on how clean energy solutions are designed and delivered
* Be part of a small, highly autonomous team with significant influence on product and technical decisions
* Collaborate with talented colleagues across product, engineering and operations
* Grow professionally in a fast-moving and international environment
* Enjoy our brand-new office at Norra Stationsgatan 93 in Stockholm. Conveniently located near public transport, with Sankt Eriksplan and Odenplan stations just a few minutes walk away.


At Aira, we champion next-generation energy as well as diversity and inclusion. We firmly believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and creativity, enhancing our ability to serve customers and communities effectively. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, cultures and perspectives to join our team. Our commitment is to uphold equality and inclusivity, fostering a collaborative environment where everyone can thrive and contribute to a better future for our people and the planet.

title: Field Application Engineer
company: アイエーアール・システムズ株式会社
description:
Are you an engineer who enjoys being in front of customers discussing embedded development and workflows? Do you have the soft skills to read a room and carry a compelling conversation? In this customer-facing role, you will act as a commercial co-driver alongside our Sales team, removing technical roadblocks and helping opportunities progress from the first conversation to adoption and long-term success.

##### **About the role**


As a Field Application Engineer (FAE), you will share the region's revenue goals and work alongside the Sales team to understand the customer’s embedded projects, tool chains, CI/CD setups, and compliance needs. By combining curiosity with active listening, you will dive deep into these setups to uncover the customer's core pain points.


Whether navigating complex technical discussions or exploring embedded software challenges, you will take full ownership of your customers to secure the deal: whether that means engineering clever technical workarounds or acting as the crucial feedback loop between the field and IAR’s product teams.

###### **You will be responsible for:**

* Technical discovery & enablement: environment validation, issue reproduction, and clear guidance that accelerates evaluations.
* Onboarding support: targeted help that keeps new customers moving toward value, including creating and delivering technical training.
* Enterprise support: trusted, tailored post-sales advice with Sales/Customer Success.
* Technical material: produce concise, useful content on a regular cadence (e.g., monthly).
##### **Who we are looking for**


You are energized by helping customers succeed and are comfortable switching between deep technical detail and clear, value-driven explanations. You hold credible conversations with R&D leaders, instinctively tailoring your approach to the specific pain points of your audience to ensure our solutions resonate with their unique needs.


Additionally, you act as a proactive partner for the sales team, independently managing your schedule, and seeking out new ways to build relationships with customers.

###### **To succeed in this role, we see that you have:**

* BSc in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering or Computer Science.
* 10 years of embedded industry experience in roles such as Field Application Engineer or Application Developer.
* Strong C and/or C++ knowledge.
* Practical understanding of developer workflows (builds, debugging, source control).
* Solid written & verbal English; confident with mixed technical/non-technical audiences.
###### **Nice-to-haves (any of the following):**

* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and headless builds; scripting (Bash/Powershell, CMake, Make etc.).
* Experience with IDEs, debuggers, RTOS, and microcontroller toolchains (Arm; RISC-V).
* Exposure to compliance contexts (e.g., Functional Safety Standards) and customer success motions (training, health checks, adoption nudges).
###### **What we offer you**


IAR is a multi-cultural and global business in a fast-growing industry, an organization where every co-worker's individual role is very important in the big picture. We welcome new ideas and take pride in our achievements. We offer you the opportunity to work in a curious, knowledge-sharing environment with a strong team spirit, where we will support you to grow both professionally and as an individual. We are problem-solvers and take on new challenges with excitement and energy.

###### **Apply Today!**


Interviews will be conducted continuously, so we encourage you to submit your application today! Please note that our team will be enjoying a well-deserved summer holiday, so application reviews and responses will resume starting **August 3rd**. We look forward to reading your application then!
