title: Backend Engineer, Berlin
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

**The Role**
 As a Backend Software Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the server-side components of our applications. You will collaborate closely with product managers, frontend developers, and other cross-functional teams to deliver robust and scalable backend services that power critical features and support rapid growth.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**Responsibilities**
* API & Service Architecture - Design and build RESTful or GraphQL APIs, microservices, and business logic using Node.js and TypeScript.
* Database Design & Integration - Model data and manage queries using Supabase and PostgreSQL, including functions, row-level security, and real-time features.
* Cloud Infrastructure - Deploy and manage services on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), particularly with Cloud Run, Cloud Functions, IAM, and Pub/Sub.
* Code Quality & Testing - Write clean, testable code using unit, integration, and end-to-end testing frameworks (e.g., Jest). Contribute to code reviews and quality assurance practices.
* Security & Reliability - Implement secure-by-default solutions for authentication, authorization, and data protection, following Clean Code and Clean Architecture principles.
* Monitoring & Operations - Set up logging, monitoring, and alerting for backend services. Own incident response and uptime improvements.
* Team Collaboration - Participate in architecture discussions, mentor team members, and document your decisions and systems for internal knowledge sharing.


**Requirements**
* 5+ years in backend engineering roles;
* Proficiency in Node.js and TypeScript;
* Strong experience with Supabase or equivalent PostgreSQL-based backend services;
* Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (e.g., GCP, AWS, Azure), including deploying and managing services using serverless compute (e.g., Cloud Run, AWS Lambda, Azure Functions);
* Solid knowledge of Clean Code and Clean Architecture practices;
* Experience with API design (REST) and cloud-native application development;
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, testing tools, and Git workflows;
* Strong grasp of security principles, data protection, and compliance best practices;
* Effective communicator and proactive team contributor;


**What benefits are we offering?**
* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

**Application Disclaimer**
 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.
   

  

 Compensation Range: €52K - €65K

title: Software Quality Engineer
description:
**What We Are Looking For**
 Our Lifesum team is growing!
   

  

 We’re looking for a
 **Software Quality Engineer** 
 who wants to lead the work to drive our quality assurance efforts, with a strong focus on testing, reliability, continuous improvement, and product excellence.
   

  

 Interacting with developers and stakeholders is very much part of the day-to-day, so communication skills are vital. We’re a small, lean team, so we’re looking for strong individuals who can take direction and run with it, challenge the status quo, and work with both team members and stakeholders proactively. You will report directly to and work closely with our Head of Technology.
   

  

 You will join a diverse team in the Tech department to ensure all elements of our platform are secure, compliant, and scalable.
   

  

 We are headquartered in Stockholm but our work reaches millions of people and we have partnerships with some of the most impactful companies in the world. That’s why our work demands us to stay flexible in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
   

  

 We hope we can be a great place to develop and advance your career. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to shape how we build and deliver Lifesum's high-quality digital products. Your work will directly impact the reliability, performance, and user experience of our solutions—ensuring that we meet the highest standards as we grow and scale.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Thoroughly test our mobile applications and web shop, helping us ship with confidence.
* You’re the last line of defence against defects and regressions making it into the user experience, but you take the context and impact of the defect into account when making a judgement call as to its severity.
* Collaborate closely with stakeholders and developers to clarify requirements, identify edge cases, and catch bugs before they reach production.
* Take ownership of the creation and maintenance of detailed, high-quality test cases and test plans for all new features and regression testing.
* Proactively flag issues in user flows and inconsistencies across platforms, advocating for quality at all stages of development.
* Help shape and document QA processes and best practices as we scale


****Who you are****
* At least 2 years of experience in manual testing, ideally within a product-focused or startup environment.
* You’ve got a sharp eye for detail and a methodical approach to finding bugs, reproducing issues, and documenting test cases.
* You’re eager to learn test automation: you don’t need to be an expert yet, but you should be curious, self-driven, and excited to pick it up.
* You’re experienced at working collaboratively with developers and product managers to define and refine requirements.
* You show excellent communication skills, both written and verbal: you're comfortable reporting bugs clearly, offering constructive feedback, and contributing to discussions.
* You are self-organised, with the ability to manage yours and your team’s workloads around the fast-paced needs of a growing startup.
* You sweat the details, but are able to balance them pragmatically against other commitments.
* Overall, you’re enthusiastic and motivated about quality, user experience, and building software that truly works for people.


**Nice to have**
* Experience with automated testing frameworks and tools like Postman for API testing.
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and tools (we use GitHub Actions).
* Basic understanding of accessibility, performance, or usability testing.


****Life at Lifesum****
 You’ll be part of a purpose-led company redefining the future of digital health and nutrition. We move fast, work together, and create space for your ideas to thrive — with real impact, every single day. And because health and wellbeing are our mission, we live it too.
   

  

 At Lifesum, you'll find:
   

  

* Hybrid work setup with our HQ in Stockholm with fixed office days (Tues-Thurs)
* Fast decision-making and real ownership — less bureaucracy, more impact
* A culture of curiosity — collaboration and engagement
* Shared wellbeing experiences — cook, train, and prioritize your health together
* Comprehensive benefits — private health insurance, personal development budget, generous wellness allowance, and more


**Who we are**
 Lifesum is a pioneering consumer health-tech company focusing on nutrition as the core focus of health and wellness. Trusted by over 65 million users worldwide, we help and empower individuals to build healthier, more sustainable eating habits that improve their lives every day.
   

  

 We are striving to build a passionate and innovative team to build user experiences that are meaningful and impactful, and working here will give you the opportunity to make a difference.
   

  

 As part of our commitment to innovation, we are becoming an AI-first company, leveraging cutting-edge technologies to revolutionize our tech stack, feature design, and development. Our journey to building the world’s leading advanced wellness platform is just beginning. We are committed to continuously improving the user experience and delivering greater value to our global Lifesum community.
   

  

 If you think you are the person for us, please reach out! Or send it to a friend who shouldn’t miss this opportunity.

title: DevSecOps engineer till Knowit Stockholm!
description:
**Varför välja oss?**
 Vill du vara med och forma framtidens teknik för en hållbar energisektor? På Knowit får du möjligheten att ta en nyckelroll i ett långsiktigt och samhällsviktigt projekt där din tekniska expertis och erfarenhet av säker programmering gör skillnad. Vi tror på att skapa en arbetsmiljö som inspirerar till samarbete och innovation, samtidigt som vi erbjuder möjligheten att utvecklas både professionellt och personligt. Hos oss blir du en del av något större – där teknik möter samhällsnytta.
   

  

**Din roll som DevSecOps Engineer**
 Som DevSecOps Engineer tar du ansvar för att utveckling sker på ett säkert sätt och i linje med de instruktioner, riktlinjer och standarder som gäller för säker mjukvaruutveckling. Du kommunicerar och utbildar dina teammedlemmar i säkerhetsfrågor.
   

  

 I din roll analyserar du behoven av struktur för styrning av användares åtkomst och behörighet, med hänsyn till verksamhetens IT-miljö, krav på säkerhet och effektivitet m.m.
   

  

**Om teamet**
 Du blir en del av ett engagerat och kunnigt team, tillsammans skapar ni lösningar som inte bara möter dagens säkerhetskrav utan också driver branschen framåt. Ni arbetar nära varandra i en agil miljö med tydligt fokus på samarbete och innovation.
   

  

**Vi tror att du:**
* Har några års erfarenhet i rollen som DevSecOps Engineer
* Har en gedigen bakgrund som utvecklare med flera års erfarenhet av systemutveckling.
* Är trygg i en fullstack-miljö med backend som specialitet och teknisk kompetens inom Java, Kafka och Kubernetes
* Har ett lösningsorienterat tankesätt och ser möjligheter där andra ser hinder.
* Är flytande i både svenska och engelska


**Om oss**
 Hos oss får du arbeta i ett sammansvetsat team där kvalitet alltid går före kvantitet. Vägen från idé till beslut är kort och du får möjlighet att ta stort ansvar och påverka både lösningar och arbetssätt. Vi erbjuder kollektivavtal, tjänstepension, försäkringar och friskvårdsbidrag, eftersom vi tycker att bra villkor är en självklar del av ett hållbart arbetsliv.

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:
**Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking, from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We’re an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don’t take ourselves too seriously. Creating and collaborating to transform the sound of streaming, content, and culture. Come join us, and let the world** 
***feel***
**your work**
 As a Site Reliability Engineer at Epidemic Sound, you will be a core member of the central platform team that builds and operates the platform the rest of Engineering ships on - keeping it reliable, scalable, and secure is what this team exists to do. This is infrastructure-flavoured software engineering: you will write the code that defines and automates the platform, and treat it as a product whose customers are the rest of Engineering. The goal is to make the reliable way the easy way - self-service paths that let product teams build and ship safely without waiting for anyone.
   

  

**Your Key Responsibilities Include**
* Build and operate the platform our services run on - GKE clusters, the controllers that extend them, and the Terraform that defines our cloud.
* Own the path from commit to production - CI/CD, GitOps, and the progressive-delivery patterns that turn a merge into a safe release.
* Strengthen the networking and routing layer - traffic management on top of the VPC, firewalls, and network policies that keep it safe and predictable.
* Govern access and guardrails - IAM across every layer, policy-as-code, and break-glass paths - so teams move fast within safe defaults rather than waiting on tickets.
* Grow reliability and observability - alert hygiene, runbooks, SLOs, and the metrics and tracing that show how the platform behaves in production.
* Enable product teams and raise the bar - make production readiness the default, and drive healthy adoption of the standards and docs you would rather share than gatekeep.


**Requirements**
* Kubernetes fundamentals: a solid grasp of controllers, core components, and CNI and networking - depth in the domain matters more than any single tool (GKE a plus).
* Infrastructure as code and delivery: Terraform, Helm or Kustomize, CI/CD and GitOps (ArgoCD), and the traffic-management and progressive-delivery mechanisms that move releases out safely.
* Networking and access: routing fundamentals, the VPC, firewall, and network-policy primitives beneath it, and IAM and access management at different levels.
* Operational depth: monitoring fundamentals (a clear view of when to reach for metrics versus tracing, and experience with an open-source observability stack), strong troubleshooting across distributed systems, and solid Unix/Linux.
* Agentic development mindset: you use AI agents actively in your own work, knowing where they add leverage and where human judgement is non-negotiable.
* Collaboration and judgement: you do your best work on large, cross-cutting projects, communicate openly, and stay opinionated but open to discussion - reaching for the right tool over your own creation.


**It would also be music to our ears if you have**
* Familiarity with GCP and an observability stack with Prometheus, Thanos, and Grafana.
* Experience running containerised platforms at scale.
* Service mesh experience with Cilium eBPF, Linkerd, or Istio.
* Familiarity with platform building blocks like cert-manager, external-secrets, or external-dns.


**Equal opportunity employer**
 We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.
   

  

**Application**
 Ready to make the world
 *feel* 
 your work? Please apply, in English.

title: Bioinformatics Engineer
description:
**BIOINFORMATICS ENGINEER** 




 Metagenomics & Computational Infrastructure
 



  





**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
 



**Employment:** 
 Full-time position
 



**Start date:** 
 Flexible, preferably in Autumn 2026.
 



  





 GenomeVault is a NatureTech company building the next generation of biodiversity intelligence through genome-wide environmental DNA (eDNA) analysis. Our work combines molecular biology, high-throughput next-generation sequencing, metagenomics, and modern computational infrastructure to help organizations measure, monitor, and manage biodiversity.
 



 We are now looking for a full-time
 **Bioinformatics Engineer** 
 to join our growing team. This hybrid role combines software and infrastructure engineering with applied bioinformatics. Approximately 60% of the role will focus on designing, building, and maintaining GenomeVault's scientific computing infrastructure, while 40% will involve bioinformatics analysis, pipeline, and method development.
 



  





**What you’ll do** 




 You will play a central role in connecting laboratory operations with high-performance computing and bioinformatics workflows. You will help establish robust, scalable infrastructure while also contributing to the analysis of complex metagenomic datasets.
 



  





**Key responsibilities** 




***Computational infrastructure (60%)***



* Design, implement, and maintain scientific computing infrastructure.
* Integrate our Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) with automated analysis workflows.
* Develop and maintain scalable Nextflow pipelines running on HPC environments.
* Build tools and databases for tracking samples, metadata, and analysis outputs.
* Improve reproducibility, automation, and data management across the organization.
* Collaborate closely with laboratory scientists to streamline the transition from sample to result.



***Bioinformatics and data analysis (40%)***



* Analyze metagenomic sequencing data from diverse environmental samples.
* Develop and optimize workflows for assembly, taxonomic profiling, genome reconstruction, and functional annotation.
* Work with microbial and eukaryotic genomes recovered from metagenomic data.
* Contribute to method development and support research projects across the company.
* Help translate biological questions into computational solutions.



  





**Who you are** 



* Passion for biodiversity and a desire to apply science to real-world environmental challenges.
* PhD or MSc in Computer Science, Bioinformatics or a related field.
* Experience designing or integrating data systems, databases, APIs, or scientific software platforms.
* Experience developing, deploying, and maintaining scalable bioinformatics pipelines for metagenomic data analysis in research or production environments.
* Strong programming skills
* Experience with workflow management systems, particularly
 **Nextflow** 
 .
* Experience working with Linux environments and high-performance computing (HPC) systems.
* Familiarity with version control and reproducible computational practices.
* A proactive mindset toward adopting and integrating AI technologies into scientific and operational workflows, and an interest in exploring how emerging AI tools can enhance efficiency, automation, and data analysis throughout the organization.
* Ability to work independently and communicate effectively in a multidisciplinary team.



**Preferred** 



* Experience recovering and analyzing
 **eukaryotic genomes from metagenomic datasets** 
 .
* Knowledge of cloud computing and infrastructure management.
* Familiarity with container technologies such as Docker and Singularity/Apptainer.
* Experience with large-scale genomics projects and data management.



  





**What we offer** 



* Join a young, mission-driven team of scientists, engineers, and entrepreneurs working to turn cutting-edge genomics into real-world biodiversity intelligence.
* The opportunity to help build the computational foundations of a rapidly growing biotechnology company.
* A highly interdisciplinary environment combining genomics, biodiversity, and software engineering.
* Significant freedom and responsibility, with the ability to shape infrastructure and analytical strategies from the ground up.
* Collaboration with scientists and engineers working at the forefront of biodiversity and genome discovery.
* Flexible and hybrid working arrangements.
* Competitive salary, pension contributions, and the possibility to participate in the company's employee stock option program.



  





**Join us** 




 If you are excited about building the infrastructure that powers next-generation metagenomics and want to work at the interface of biology, software engineering, and biodiversity science, we would love to hear from you.
 



  





 Please send your CV and a short cover letter describing your background and interest in the position to
 **contact@genomevault.net** 
 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Embeddedutvecklare till stridsflygplan
description:
*Vi tror att framsteg och möjligheter skapas bäst tillsammans. Välkommen till en plats där samarbete gör skillnad.*
**Din roll**
 Vill du arbeta nära tekniken som bokstavligen sitter i pilotens händer, och samtidigt bidra till något som gör verklig skillnad? Som mjukvaruutvecklare inom Aeronautics blir du en viktig del av utvecklingen av Gripen C/D och dess cockpitlösningar.
   

  

 I den här rollen får du kombinera djup teknisk förståelse med ett helhetsperspektiv. Du arbetar både med detaljer i enskilda mjukvarukomponenter och hur de samverkar i hela systemet och flygplanet. Fokus ligger på mjukvara som driver HMI (Human Machine Interface) och registreringsfunktioner i cockpit, där prestanda, tillförlitlighet och användbarhet är avgörande.
   

  

 Ditt arbete kommer bland annat att innebära att:
   

  

* Ta fram krav, designa, implementera och testa nya funktioner
* Arbeta med verifiering och dokumentation i olika utvecklingsfaser
* Vidareutveckla och optimera befintliga lösningar
* Integrera och funktionstesta mjukvara i labb, simulatorer och riggar


 Du blir en del av ett team där samarbete är centralt – både internt och tillsammans med externa partners. Tillsammans säkerställer ni lösningar som fungerar i verkligheten, där varje detalj räknas.
   

  

**Din profil**
 Du drivs av att lösa komplexa problem och vill se hur din kod gör skillnad i ett större sammanhang. Du trivs i samarbeten och är nyfiken på att förstå både tekniska detaljer och systemets helhet.
   

  

 Vi ser att du har:
   

  

* God erfarenhet av hårdvarunära embeddedutveckling, gärna i C/C++, Pascal eller ADA
* Civilingenjörsexamen inom exempelvis datateknik, elektroteknik, teknisk fysik, media eller spelutveckling
* Förmåga att anpassa ditt arbetssätt utifrån olika situationer och utvecklingsfaser
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i både tal och skrift


 Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av realtidssystem eller arbete i komplexa, säkerhetskritiska miljöer.
   

  

 Som person är du analytisk, engagerad och lösningsorienterad – och du motiveras av att bidra till teknik som gör skillnad för människor och samhällen.
   

  

*Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.*
**Vad blir du en del av**
 Upptäck oändliga möjligheter. Anta utmaningar, skapa smarta innovationer och uppgradera dina förmågor. Det här är en plats för kluriga tänkare, modiga pionjärer och alla däremellan. Tillsammans uppnår vi det extraordinära, var och en tar med sitt unika perspektiv. Varje bidrag räknas.
   

  

 Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 26,100 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.
   

  

 Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.

title: ML Research Engineer - Narrative & AI Systems
description:
As a ML Research Engineer on Embark's Machine Learning team, you will help shape the future of storytelling tools and systems for video games. We believe modern AI can unlock entirely new creative workflows for narrative design, quest building, lore creation, and player-facing reactivity. Our goal is to investigate how we can design new tools and systems that enable creators to be more ambitious, collaborate more effectively, and explore ideas with less friction rather than simply automating existing workflows.
   

  

 In this role, you will research, prototype, and build AI-driven narrative systems - exploring what's possible before optimizing for production. You'll work closely with writers, and designers to explore different levels of AI agency to intentionally design where AI agency makes sense and where it doesn't.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who is genuinely passionate about narrative and storytelling, has a research mindset, and is curious about what new AI techniques can enable in games.
   

  

**Example Of Responsibilities**
* Build gameplay systems and tools that enable new narrative workflows and faster iteration for creators
* Collaborate with designers and narrative teams to understand creative needs and turn them into intuitive tooling
* Research and prototype AI-driven narrative systems - quest generation, reactive dialogue, authoring tools, and beyond
* Design and run lightweight experiments to evaluate creative AI applications
* Work with writers and designers to explore and prototype AI-native gameplay mechanics that preserve authorial intent while enabling emergence and reactivity
* Prototype experimental features quickly, then help mature the best ideas into robust solutions
* Help design and evaluate different degrees of AI autonomy, balancing creative ambition, reliability, and control
* Help define what 'good' looks like when AI is involved in storytelling


**We would love if you have**
* A research or engineering background with hands-on experience in ML or LLM applications
* A genuine passion for narrative: games, film, interactive fiction, or otherwise
* Curiosity and comfort working in ambiguous, exploratory problem spaces
* A creator mindset: you enjoy building things that make other people more effective
* Comfort collaborating with writers and designers and translating creative goals into technical solutions
* Professional English communication skills


**Additionally, It's a Bonus If You Have**
* Experience working in Unreal Engine or with C++
* Experience with narrative systems, quest pipelines, or other creator-driven game features
* A background in ML, NLP, generative models, or evaluation methodologies
* Python experience, or interest in working across engine + services/tooling


 At Embark we offer competitive salaries, a generous profit-sharing program, and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
   

  

 We welcome people from all backgrounds and are looking forward to reading more about you (in English)!

title: Senior iOS Developer
description:
**About Ubiquiti**
 At Ubiquiti Inc., we create technology platforms for Businesses, Smart Homes, and Internet Service Providers, driven by our goal to connect everyone, everywhere. To date, Ubiquiti has shipped over 100 million devices worldwide, from ISP networking products to next generation of IT solutions. Our growth is made possible by the dedicated team of hundreds behind the scenes. From software developers and product managers to designers and strategists, Team UI is driven to achieve our common goal: Rethinking IT. At Ubiquiti, you’ll heighten your potential and broaden your horizons - all while shaping the future of connectivity.
   

  

**Join forces with us on our mission to build a better IT industry. We are currently looking for a highly skilled Senior iOS Developer to our team in Stockholm, Sweden.**
***Please note that applicants must live in Sweden and hold a valid work permit at the time of application to be considered for this role.***
**Responsibilities**
* Create state of the art mobile apps that are both powerful and easy to use with elite UX applied
* Work in an agile environment, individually and with a team of developers across multiple R&D sites globally


**Requirements**
* At least 5 years of experience in a similar role
* Proven track record of releasing and maintaining successful iOS apps
* Ability to write efficient code, with knowledge of performance implications, such as CPU and memory usage
* Extensive knowledge of Swift, SwiftUI and Core Data
* Experience with RESTful APIs
* Proficient understanding of Git versioning tool
* Passion for design and great UX, understanding Apple´s human interface guidelines
* Highly proficient in English (written and spoken)


**Nice to have**
* Experience with SwiftLint, SwiftFormat or similar tools
* Experience with WebRTC and BLE
* Knowledge of networking


**Benefits**
* International work environment
* Competitive compensation package including wellness allowance, occupational pension, medical insurance, private health insurance, life insurance, and work-injury insurance
* Five weeks of holiday per year
* Flexible work hours and hybrid work model
* Modern office space located in Epicenter (Hötorget)
* Access to all the latest Ubiquiti products and prototypes for home use and tests


***Please note that a background check will be conducted in the final stage of the recruitment process***

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
***This is us***
 At Avenga, we believe that human creativity empowers technology that matters. Operating globally, our 6000+ specialists provide a full spectrum of services, including business and tech advisory, enterprise solutions, CX, UX and Ul design, managed services, product development, and software development.
   

  

***This is the job***
 In
 **Sweden** 
 within the
 **Banking** 
 industry, we are actively seeking a professional to strengthen our team dedicated to
 **Data Engineering** 
 .
   

  

***This is you***
* Several years of experience as a Senior Data Engineer, including both maintenance and new development
* Strong expertise in SQL and hands-on experience building and maintaining ETL processes, preferably using SSIS
* Proven experience with modern data platforms in Azure, ideally including Databricks
* Experience with data transformation and modeling tools, preferably dbt
* Strong interest in data from a business and user perspective, with an understanding of the importance of reliable and well-structured data
* Proactive, communicative, and comfortable working in cross-functional teams
* Experience from banking, financial services, or other regulated industries is an asset
* Fluent in Swedish and English


***This is your role***
* Manage, develop, and optimize the existing SQL-based data warehouse, including ETL processes built in SSIS
* Ensure stable operations, high data quality, and accurate business logic to support reporting, analytics, and decision-making
* Design and develop data pipelines in a modern Azure environment, with a focus on Databricks and scalable architectures
* Transform and model data (e.g., using dbt or similar tools), ensuring quality, traceability, and reusability
* Contribute to establishing frameworks, ways of working, and best practices for the future data platform (e.g., version control, security, documentation, and coding standards)
* Work closely with BI teams, analysts, and business stakeholders to understand data usage and translate requirements into sustainable technical solutions


***What awaits you at Avenga?***
*At Avenga, everyone matters. We provide equal opportunities in recruitment, career development, and leadership, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion, or any other characteristic. We are committed to fostering a work environment where our diverse community of employees, candidates, and business partners actively shapes our growth. By bringing together people from different backgrounds and experiences, we build a workplace where everyone feels free to be themselves while honoring the boundaries of others.*

title: Requirement Engineer ( AI Engineer)
description:
TRATON is a group of strong brands with a shared mission: transforming transportation together to create the future of sustainable transport solutions. Within TRATON, we include MAN, Scania, Volkswagen Truck & Bus, and International. As part of a global team of industry experts, you get to think bigger, experience more, and reach further. Together, we have the power to transform transportation - Let´s make a difference together. Find out more: www.traton.com
   

  

 Our values – customer first, respect, team spirit, responsibility, and elimination of waste – are at the heart of everything we do.
   

  

**Assignment**
 We are looking for a requirement engineer to work with our in-house developed battery and thermal control systems for electrified vehicles. You will be part of a global agile team of 8 skilled and passionate colleagues. Your main work will be writing requirements on system level.
   

  

 The work is characterized by networking and collaboration with other team members but also other competences in our organization. You will perform own deliveries as well as supporting the organization with your competence in requirement topics.
   

  

 Our organization is highly focusing on AI usage and the role can potentially (not a must), for the right candidate, be combined with part time work on developing and prototyping tools that solve engineering problems and improve team productivity, not only within this area of competence.
   

  

**Your profile**
 You have a master degree in computer science, information technology, engineering physics or equivalent and +5 years of experience from working with vehicle embedded systems. Previous experience from working as a requirement engineer is required, and other experience from working as a system engineer, software developer or test developer is meritorious as well as domain knowledge in battery and thermal control systems. Knowledge in functional safety (ISO26262) is also meritorious.
   

  

 Curiosity in AI developing AI agents and LLM-based solutions, with solid Python programming skills to build automations, integrate APIs, and rapidly prototype tools.
   

  

 You are comfortable with taking lead in the work and enjoy involving your colleagues. We are a large organization with a great deal of cross-functional collaboration and your skills in communication and building relationships will be appreciated. Good knowledge in English is required.
   

  

**We offer**
 At TRATON, you are part of something bigger. Together with colleagues across our global brands, you will contribute to shaping the future of sustainable transportation.
   

  

 We offer an environment where collaboration across teams and borders is key, giving you the opportunity to learn, grow, and make a real impact. Working at TRATON means being close to innovation, business decisions, and industry transformation — all while being supported in your professional development.
   

  

 You’ll benefit from competitive employment conditions, including wellness allowance, bonus, flexible working hours, and company car options. At the same time, you’ll be part of a culture that values openness, teamwork, and continuous learning.
   

  

**Application**
 If you are interested, please apply before 2026-08-15. Your application should include a CV. We will review the applications and conduct interviews continuously. A background check will be conducted for this position. For more information, please contact Emma Skoglund, Head of Electrification Management Systems, emma.skoglund@scania.com.

title: Byt till det bättre konsultbolaget
description:
Väntar du på nästa konsultuppdrag som aldrig kommer? Trött på garantilön eller 70/30–modellen, samtidigt som budgetarna skärs ner och sammanhållningen försvinner? Då är det hög tid att byta till HiQ i Stockholm!
   

  

**Om rollen:**
 Vi söker dig som är IT-konsult idag, men som vill komma till det självklara valet av konsultbolag istället. Hos oss blir du en central del av team i samhällsbärande uppdrag där teknik faktiskt gör skillnad på riktigt inom branscher som bank, finans, retail, offentlig sektor och industri. Utöver det kliver du in i ett kulturdrivet bolag som inte bara har otrolig utsikt över Gamla stan, utan som också har riktigt kul tillsammans på jobbet. Här börjar vi gärna måndagen med frukost, kör ett lunchpass i vår egna träningslokal, och avslutar dagen med ett tech-event eller en AW på vår takterass.
   

  

**Du kommer att:**
* Arbeta i uppdrag där dina leveranser märks, både hos kund och slutanvändare
* Ha chefer och kollegor som faktiskt ser hur du har det i uppdraget och som stöttar när du vill byta spår, växa eller ta mer ansvar
* Bli en del av vårt härliga Stockholmsgäng i lokaler med en utsikt som gör det lite roligare att gå till jobbet
* Vara med på techevents, after works och aktiviteter som bygger både gemenskap och kunskap


**Vi tror att du har:**
* Erfarenhet som konsult
* Flera års teknisk erfarenhet, gärna inom backend (Java, C++, .NET), DevOps/plattform, cybersäkerhet eller AI
* Svenskt medborgarskap samt flytande i svenska och engelska
* Ett arbetssätt där du är nyfiken, tar initiativ, delar med dig av din erfarenhet och gärna hjälper andra att lyckas
* Förmåga att snabbt sätta dig in i nya domäner, skapa förtroende hos kund och ta ansvar för leverans och kvalitet


**Om HiQ**
 Sedan 1995 har HiQ varit konsultbolaget som förenklat världen för både kunder och slutanvändare med hjälp av teknik, design och kommunikation. HiQ har alltid fokuserat på två saker: att ha kul på jobbet och att leverera lösningar utöver förväntningar - det är vad som driver oss framåt! Vi HiQare är stolta över att ha en kultur som präglas av
 **resultat** 
 ,
 **ansvar, enkelhet** 
 och
 **glädje** 
 . Vi tror på att arbeta tillsammans för att nå våra gemensamma mål och strävar efter att hjälpa varandra att fortsätta växa och utvecklas. Hos oss får du utöver chansen att utveckla din egen kompetens också möjlighet att lära, leda och inspirera dina kollegor.
   

  

*Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!*

title: Analytics Engineer
description:
Senior / Mid-Level Analytics Engineer
 



 Based in Stockholm
 



 Salary - Flexible but ideally landing between 50-65K SEK, dependent on experience.
 



  





 We’re working with a fast-growing, product-led company operating in the consumer space, building a modern data platform to power decision-making across the entire business. They’re now looking for an Analytics Engineer to play a key role in shaping how data is defined, modelled, and used - partnering closely with stakeholders across product, commercial, and operations.
 



  





 Why this role might be of interest?
 



 This isn’t a traditional data engineering role focused on pipelines or infrastructure. Instead, you’ll sit at the heart of the business, owning how data is transformed into meaningful, trusted datasets that teams rely on every day.
 



  





 You’ll have real influence over:
 



  




* How core business metrics are defined
* How data is structured and modelled across the company
* How stakeholders interact with and trust data



  





 It’s a highly collaborative role where strong communication and curiosity are just as important as technical ability.
 



  





 What will you be doing?
 


* Transform raw data into clean, well-structured datasets for analytics and reporting
* Design and maintain scalable, well-documented data models
* Work closely with non-technical stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into data solutions
* Identify inconsistencies or gaps in data and proactively improve data quality
* Contribute to best practices around analytics engineering and data transformation



  





 Tech environment:
 


* Strong focus on SQL and data transformation
* DBT as a core tool for modelling and transformation
* Cloud data warehouse environment (e.g. Snowflake or similar)
* Exposure to orchestration tools like Airflow is beneficial



  





 What we are looking for?
 



  




* Experience working with data in an analytics-focused role (analytics engineer, data analyst, or similar)
* Strong SQL skills and a passion for working with data
* A genuine interest in data modelling and structuring data for business use
* Detail-oriented mindset with a focus on data quality and consistency
* Comfortable working cross-functionally with a variety of stakeholders
* Clear and thoughtful communication style



  





 Nice to have...
 


* Experience with DBT
* Some experience with Python
* Familiarity with modern data stack tools



  





 Who does this suit?
 



 This role is ideal for someone who enjoys being close to the business and wants to have a direct impact on how data is understood and used - rather than focusing purely on backend infrastructure.
 



  





 It could suit:
 


* A data analyst looking to move further upstream into data modelling
* An analytics engineer looking for more ownership and stakeholder interaction



  





 If you're interested in a role where you can shape the way a company thinks about data - without being confined to pure engineering - this is a genuinely exciting opportunity to make an impact.
